label: Brannik Emberjudge

sa:identity.label: The Ember Judge
sa:identity.description: A tribunal enforcer and oath-burner who reads guilt like forge heat and carries a relic blade that looks far too large for his frame.

schema:description: Brannik Emberjudge is a compact gnome with a severe stare, a scarred face, and the posture of someone who has already heard every excuse and found them all wanting. He serves as a tribunal enforcer, oath-auditor, and occasional field judge for disputes too dangerous, too old, or too politically inconvenient to resolve in daylight. His signature weapon is a radiant crescent relic that glows like a furnace edge, making him easy to recognize and harder to lie to. Brannik does not seek cruelty for its own sake; he seeks finality. In the right hands, that makes him a useful ally. In the wrong ones, it makes him a recurring problem.

sa:nature.appearance: Brannik is a stocky gnome with a thick red beard, weathered skin, and a face that looks permanently set for cross-examination. The glowing crescent weapon above him reads like a ritual seal made into steel and light.
sa:nature.demeanor: He speaks with tight control and wastes no words. Even at rest, he looks like he is weighing testimony.
sa:nature.presence: He is memorable because he is small, exact, and impossible to ignore once he has chosen a target.
sa:nature.weapon: The crescent relic is part badge, part execution tool, part inherited judgment. He treats it as proof that sentence and sentence carry equal weight.

sa:mechanics.method: He investigates first, listens second, and strikes only when he believes the record is complete enough to stand in court or in blood.
sa:mechanics.philosophy: A vow is not a promise. It is a structure. Break it, and the whole edifice deserves collapse.
sa:mechanics.field_role: He can appear as auditor, rival, protector, witness, or executioner depending on how the party has behaved.
sa:mechanics.recurring_use: He works best as a character who returns whenever contracts are violated, evidence is incomplete, or a debt still has a name attached to it.

sa:relationships.Forge-Syndicate: A useful but not fully trusted enforcer whose authority comes from competence and fear of precedent.
sa:relationships.oathbreakers: His preferred quarry; he treats them as structural damage to the world rather than simple criminals.
sa:relationships.lawful allies: He respects people who keep records, honor terms, and admit fault before the blade comes out.
sa:relationships.players: He may become a patron if they are reliable, a witness if they are honest, or a relentless complication if they are not.

sa:quotes: I do not punish. I conclude.
Your explanation is organized. That is not the same as true.
A broken vow still has shape. I am here to cut it down cleanly.
